name: South African Republic (Transvaal, 1858–1902)

colors.symbolism: from the Dutch tricolour at the fly; freedom
the hoist band, added to distinguish the Vierkleur from the Dutch national flag; popularly associated with the Voortrekkers and the Afrikaner connection to the land

description: A vertical green band at the hoist (one quarter of the flag's length) and at the fly three equal horizontal stripes — red, white and blue from top to bottom — reproducing the Dutch national flag.
history: Designed by Reverend Dirk van der Hoff and first hoisted at Potchefstroom on 6 January 1857; officially adopted by the Volksraad of the Zuid-Afrikaansche Republiek on 18 February 1858. The 'Vierkleur' (four-colour) added a green hoist band to the Dutch tricolour, expressing the republic's Dutch-Afrikaner identity. It was the flag of the Boer Republic until the British annexation following the Second Boer War in 1902. The design was revived as one of the three small flags embedded in the central stripe of the 1928–1994 South African flag.

features.symbolism: the Dutch tricolour at the fly, expressing the republic's Netherlands heritage
the green hoist band that distinguishes the Vierkleur from the plain Dutch national flag
